title: Awesome-Audio-LLM item
description: Schema for items/*.json. Each file describes one paper, model, benchmark, dataset, or survey. README.md is regenerated from these files by format_input.py.

Category.description: Top-level README section. Must be one of the canonical categories.

Type.description: Short free-form descriptor (e.g. Model, Benchmark, Survey, Method, Dataset Resource, Research, Multimodal Language Model, Speech Recognition Model, Infrastructure).

Abbreviation.description: Short canonical name (e.g. SPIRIT LM, Audio Flamingo 2). Must be unique across items/. The JSON filename should match this slug.

Title.description: Full paper title or release title.

Time.description: YYYY-MM of the arXiv submission or release. Used to sort the timeline image and README sections.

Affiliation.description: Comma-separated affiliation list. Empty string allowed.

Author.description: Comma-separated author list, or a team name (e.g. 'Microsoft Phi-4-Mini Team'). Empty string allowed.

Paper_Link.description: Full URL (arXiv preferred) or empty string.

Demo_Link.description: Demo site URL or empty string.

Other_Link.description: Project page, conference page, etc. Empty string allowed.

Audio_Input.description: Whether the work accepts audio input. Yes/No preferred; empty string and '-' are tolerated for legacy entries.

Audio_Output.description: Whether the work produces audio output. Yes/No preferred; empty string and '-' are tolerated for legacy entries.

Language.description: Free-form language descriptor (English, Multilingual, Mandarin, ...). Empty string allowed.

Description.description: One- to two-sentence summary. Empty string tolerated for legacy entries; required for new entries.
